items.q: Which seed is ground to make tahini?
items.explanation: Tahini is a smooth paste made from sesame seeds and is a key ingredient in foods such as hummus and halva.

items.q: Lightning is seen before thunder is heard because light travels faster than sound.
items.explanation: Light reaches observers almost instantly over typical storm distances, while sound waves move much more slowly through air.

items.q: In bowling, what is the name for three strikes in a row?
items.explanation: The term turkey is an old prize-based nickname that stuck long after actual holiday birds stopped being awarded.

items.q: What spice is made from dried flower buds of a tropical tree?
items.explanation: Cloves come from the flower buds of an evergreen tree in the myrtle family and have a strong, warm flavor used in both sweet and savory dishes.

items.q: What art technique arranges cut or torn paper pieces onto a surface to make an image?
items.explanation: Collage became especially prominent in early twentieth-century modern art, where artists combined paper with photographs, labels, and other materials.

items.q: Which explorer is associated with the first expedition to circumnavigate the globe, even though he died before it was completed?
items.explanation: Magellan did not survive the voyage, but the expedition he began completed the first recorded circumnavigation in 1522.

items.q: Bananas are classified as berries in botanical terms.
items.explanation: In botany, berries develop from a single ovary and can contain multiple seeds, which includes bananas.

items.q: Which company created the Walkman portable cassette player?
items.explanation: Sony's Walkman line helped make personal portable music listening a global habit long before digital players appeared.

items.q: What do you call a person who translates spoken language in real time?
items.explanation: Interpreters work with spoken language, while translators usually work with written text.

items.q: What plant tissue carries water from the roots to the leaves?
items.explanation: Xylem moves water and dissolved minerals upward, while phloem mainly distributes sugars made by photosynthesis.

items.q: What fermented Korean side dish is commonly made from napa cabbage and chili?
items.explanation: Kimchi has many regional varieties, but cabbage-based versions seasoned with chili are among the most widely known.

items.q: The metric prefix kilo means one hundred.
items.explanation: In SI units, kilo denotes a factor of one thousand, while hecto denotes one hundred.

items.q: What term describes a word borrowed from another language?
items.explanation: English contains many loanwords, ranging from algebra and karaoke to bungalow and safari.

items.q: What name is given to the line dividing Earth's daylight side from its night side at a given moment?
items.explanation: Astronomers use terminator for the moving boundary between light and darkness on a planetary body.

items.q: Which novelist created the spy George Smiley?
items.explanation: George Smiley appears across several of le Carre's Cold War novels, including Tinker Tailor Soldier Spy.

items.q: What web image format stands for Scalable Vector Graphics?
items.explanation: Because SVG stores shapes and paths as vectors rather than pixels, it can scale cleanly without becoming blurry.

items.q: Pure water has a pH of 7 at 25 degrees Celsius.
items.explanation: At standard laboratory temperature, pure water is neutral because hydrogen and hydroxide ion concentrations are equal.

items.q: What universal character standard is used in computing to encode scripts such as Arabic, Devanagari, and Korean?
items.explanation: Unicode assigns code points to characters so the same text can be represented consistently across platforms and languages.

items.q: Which playwright wrote The Cherry Orchard?
items.explanation: The Cherry Orchard premiered in 1904 and was the last play Chekhov completed.

items.q: What term describes a written statement confirmed by oath for use as evidence?
items.explanation: Affidavits are commonly used in court filings and administrative proceedings when formal sworn testimony is not taken in person.

items.q: Which musical mode matches a natural minor scale with a raised sixth degree?
items.explanation: Dorian often appears in folk and jazz because the raised sixth gives it a brighter color than the natural minor.

items.q: The Sargasso Sea is defined by surrounding ocean currents rather than coastlines.
items.explanation: Unlike most seas, the Sargasso Sea lies in the North Atlantic and is bounded mainly by circulating currents.

items.q: What boundary marks the point around a black hole beyond which light cannot escape?
items.explanation: An event horizon is not a solid surface; it is the threshold beyond which every future path leads inward.

items.q: Which philosopher wrote Discourse on Method?
items.explanation: Descartes published Discourse on Method in 1637 as a preface to scientific essays, linking philosophical method with mathematical reasoning.

items.q: What principle in contract law requires that something of value be exchanged between parties?
items.explanation: Under common-law contract doctrine, consideration is the bargained-for exchange that distinguishes an enforceable promise from a mere gift.

items.q: Which novelist created the fictional Yoknapatawpha County?
items.explanation: Faulkner returned to Yoknapatawpha across novels and stories, turning the county into a richly layered fictional version of Mississippi.

items.q: In SQL, an INNER JOIN returns all rows from the left table even when there is no match in the right table.
items.explanation: An INNER JOIN returns only rows where the join condition matches in both tables; unmatched left rows require a LEFT JOIN.

items.q: Which Roman magistrate was normally one of two officials who jointly held the Republic's highest annual office?
items.explanation: The consulship combined military and civic authority, and the paired office was meant to limit the power of any one officeholder.

items.q: What is the smallest meaningful unit of language called?
items.explanation: A word can contain more than one morpheme, such as the plural ending in cats or the prefix and suffix in unhappiness.

items.q: Which emperor presided over the Diet of Worms in 1521?
items.explanation: Martin Luther was summoned before the assembly during Charles V's reign, making the Diet of Worms a defining moment of the Reformation.
